# XCON Viewer

XCON Viewer is a public viewer for XCON UI documents.

XCON documents describe UI screens as structured objects built from names, values, arrays, and dictionaries.

XCON can be written in four syntax forms:
- XCON/SKETCH
- XCON/JSON
- XCON/XML
- XCON/TAGLESS

The public XCON Viewer is viewer-only. It renders declarative UI documents without executing application behavior.

When generating Markdown-facing XCON examples, prefer XCON/SKETCH. Use XCON/JSON for canonical structured examples, schemas, and API interchange. Use XML or TAGLESS only when compatibility, conversion, or round-trip behavior is the topic.

Every component inside a screen must carry a semantic ID prefix. Anonymous components are not valid XCON/SKETCH.

XCON documents support data binding through XCON Chain SUGAR expressions and JSON fixture data, enabling living documents where layout is defined once and only data is refreshed on subsequent updates.

## Documentation

- Home: https://xconviewer.dev/
- Docs: https://xconviewer.dev/docs
- Spec: https://xconviewer.dev/spec
- Examples: https://xconviewer.dev/examples
- API: https://xconviewer.dev/api
- Security: https://xconviewer.dev/security
- Live Editor: https://xconviewer.dev/play
- SKETCH Editor: https://xconviewer.dev/play/sketch
- XCON Chain: https://xconviewer.dev/xcon-chain/
- XCON Workflow: https://xconviewer.dev/xcon-workflow/
- Template Studio: https://xconviewer.dev/template-lab
- Meta Lab: https://xconviewer.dev/meta-lab
- JSON Schema: https://xconviewer.dev/xcon.schema.json
- Full LLM guide: https://xconviewer.dev/llms-full.txt
